title: campanula cichoracea
intro: Campanula cichoracea, also known as rampion, is a perennial plant belonging to the Campanulaceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant's roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, as well as their ability to lower blood pressure and reduce fever. The health benefits of Campanula cichoracea include its potential to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and bloating, as well as its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Campanula cichoracea can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is sometimes consumed raw in salads or used as a culinary ingredient.
